item.title: Infamous cartel boss "El Mayo" Zambada sentenced to life in U.S. prison
item.description: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada ordered torture and plotted murders as he Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán grew the Sinaloa cartel into the world's largest drug trafficking organization, prosecutors said.

item.title: Why oil prices are climbing again
item.description: Crude oil prices surged once again after the U.S.-Iran ceasefire collapsed. CBS News contributor Javier David has more details.

item.title: F-16s intercept multiple planes in restricted airspace over New Jersey
item.description: F-16 fighter jets intercepted multiple planes that flew through restricted airspace over Bedminster and East Rutherford, New Jersey, on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. vows to "swiftly punish" Iran as at least 3 troops killed
item.description: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

item.title: Suspect in custody after explosion in downtown Manhattan, FBI says
item.description: A suspect is in custody after an incendiary device went off outside 26 Federal Plaza in downtown Manhattan, the FBI said. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more details.

item.title: More details on explosion outside federal building in Manhattan
item.description: Former NYPD Sgt. Felipe Rodriguez joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss the explosion outside of 26 Federal Plaza in downtown Manhattan, which houses dozens of federal agencies.

item.title: Suspect in custody after explosion in trash can outside NYC federal building
item.description: NYPD sources said fireworks were found in a garbage can and a man was taken into custody outside the federal building in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Arizona gears up for primary races, including GOP contest for governor
item.description: Several candidates in Arizona are seeking the GOP nomination to take on Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs on Tuesday. Meanwhile, the state is also holding primary contests for congressional seats. CBS News' Fin Gomez has the latest.

item.title: Can an authorized user be sued for unpaid credit card debt?
item.description: Being added as a card user can boost your credit, but does it also mean your bank account is on the hook?

item.title: Tate brothers to appear in Miami court on U.K. rape, sex trafficking charges
item.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate are set to appear in a Miami courtroom after U.S. Marshals Service arrested them to face rape and sex trafficking charges in the United Kingdom. CBS News' Cristian Benavides has the latest.

item.title: The U.S. labor market may soon face a new crisis: Too few workers
item.description: A severe labor shortage is set to emerge over the next decade amid a wave of baby boomer retirements and a shrinking younger workforce.

item.title: Pentagon ID's 2 soldiers killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: Nolan Wells' funeral to be held as questions swirl around Mississippi teen's death
item.description: Funeral services are set to take place Monday for Nolan Wells, the 18-year-old from Mississippi who was found dead after a July Fourth boating trip with friends. CBS News' Skyler Henry has the latest.

item.title: Soldiers who died in Iranian strike on U.S. base in Jordan were 19 and 25 years old, military says
item.description: The Pentagon identified the U.S. soldiers who died Friday in an Iranian strike on a U.S. base in Jordan as 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales and 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan. A third service member is still missing in action. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Nikole Killion have the latest.

item.title: "Dear David": Trailblazer's legacy, coach celebrated and hospital network spreads hope
item.description: "In this week's "Beg-Knows America," CBS News contributor David Begnaud shares three inspiring stories. First, the descendant of a Civil Rights trailblazer gets a big surprise. Plus, a coach who inspired so many others is honored, and a hospital network helps spread messages of hope.

item.title: How long will Andy Burnham last as U.K. prime minister, the 7th in a decade?
item.description: Journalist and podcast host Andrew Neil joins "CBS Morning News" to discuss Britain's new prime minister Andy Burnham.

item.title: Who notifies creditors and debt collectors when a borrower dies?
item.description: A loved one's debt doesn't disappear after death, so it's important to know who handles creditor communications.

item.title: AOC, Sanders campaign for Michigan Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez rallied with Michigan Senate hopeful Abdul El-Sayed, who faces Haley Stevens in an Aug. 4 primary. CBS News contributor Joel Payne and Alex Vogel, CEO of the Vogel Group, join with more.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Christopher Nolan talks "The Odyssey," its authenticity and challenges faced creating the film
item.description: Oscar-winning director Christopher Nolan talks about his latest movie, "The Odyssey," which was shot in six countries. He tells "CBS Mornings" about what it was like working with the star-studded cast and challenges faced when creating the film.

item.title: Maine Democratic Senate race narrows as candidates rally behind Troy Jackson
item.description: The crowded Maine Senate race to replace Graham Platner has narrowed as Democrats rallied around former state Senate President Troy Jackson in the leadup to the party's nominating convention on Saturday.

item.title: Spain defeats Argentina after defensive battle to win World Cup
item.description: Spain won its second World Cup title ever, defeating Argentina in extra time 1-0. Nicole Valdes has the highlights.

item.title: Victims of Iranian-sponsored terror want restitution as part of peace deal
item.description: A coalition of victims impacted by terrorism is demanding Iran pay restitution before any funds are returned or unfrozen as part of a peace deal.

item.title: What it's like inside Ukraine during a massive Russian ballistic missile attack
item.description: Russia targeted Kyiv, Ukraine, in one of its largest missile assaults of the war over the weekend, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said. CBS News' Aidan Stretch has the latest from Kyiv.

item.title: Tick bites have soared this year. Here's why, and how to protect yourself
item.description: Emergency room visits for tick bites are the highest in nearly a decade as ticks spread into new parts of the U.S. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Celine Gounder explains why we're seeing more ticks, and when a Lyme disease vaccine could be available.

item.title: Fire captain, wife, 3 sons found dead after flash flood in Utah canyon
item.description: The victims were a captain in the Provo, Utah, fire department, his wife and their sons, according to fire officials.

item.title: Gas prices top $4 a gallon again as U.S.-Iran conflict escalates
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gas reached $4.003 on Monday, the first time in over a month it has crossed that threshold, AAA data shows.

item.title: Taylor Farms lettuce sample was false positive for cyclospora, FDA says
item.description: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing for Cyclospora."

item.title: Student loan defaults surging in wake of COVID-tied payments pause expiration
item.description: Defaults on student loans have surged across the United States, reaching record levels as borrowers struggle to keep up with payments.

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.
